title: Toroidal Wrapping, Object Bounds, Viewports, Resize, and Scaling · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed playtest review reconstructs toroidal wrapping, object bounds, viewports, resize, and scaling from revision, seed, input frames, simulation snapshots, draw records, audio cues, accessibility settings, profiles, package artifacts, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for seam-safe logical viewport and resize policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defe…
objectives: Choose center, radius, Rect, FRect, and visual bounds deliberately and keep float world state separate from raster bounds.
Wrap complete object bounds with modular arithmetic and test overshoot across corners and large time steps.
Compare shortest toroidal displacement or ghost proxies so collisions remain continuous across seams.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Center Vs Bounds · changed case · Review Center Vs Bounds
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Asteroids team handles case 8739 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to wrap full object bounds across seams while preserving logical world state through resize and display scaling; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose center, radius, Rect, FRect, and visual bounds deliberately and keep float world state separate from raster bounds.

stimulus.title: Resize Dpi case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review Asteroids team handles case 9453 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to wrap full object bounds across seams while preserving logical world state through resize and display scaling; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle resize, minimize, restore, high-DPI, fullscreen, and zero extents without corrupting world state.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Window size and drawable pixel size are always identical.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle resize, minimize, restore, high-DPI, fullscreen, and zero extents without corrupting world state. Artifact under review: seam-safe logical viewport and resize policy.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Resize Dpi, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Resize Dpi
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Resize Dpi: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Asteroids team handles case 7834 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to wrap full object bounds across seams while preserving logical world state through resize and display scaling; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle resize, minimize, restore, high-DPI, fullscreen, and zero extents without corrupting world state.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Resize Dpi.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Resize Dpi, keep Resize Dpi connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Handle resize, minimize, restore, high-DPI, fullscreen, and zero extents without corrupting world state.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Resize Dpi, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Resize Dpi precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Resize Dpi, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A review Asteroids team handles case 7834 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to wrap full object bounds across seams while preserving logical world state through resize and display scaling; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle resize, minimize, restore, high-DPI, fullscreen, and zero extents without corrupting world state.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Resize Dpi, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Window size and drawable pixel size are always identical.
